seo.title: Writers Guild · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Writers Guild supports field reports, publishing, translations, and public knowledge work across the network.

guilds.name: Writers Guild
guilds.summary: Publishing, storytelling, research, and public sensemaking across the Greenpill network.
guilds.description: The Writers Guild supports essays, field reports, translations, publishing workflows, and editorial work across chapters and guilds. Public sources show Greenpill's Paragraph publication, the long-running podcast, Garden writing needs, and a network-wide push toward clearer public knowledge infrastructure.
guilds.oneliner: Stories, translations, field reports, and public knowledge work for the network.

outputs.label: Field reports
outputs.summary: Chapter and guild updates that turn local work into public learning.

outputs.label: Translations
outputs.summary: Translation work around Greenpill books and chapter resources, including Brasil's Portuguese work.

outputs.label: Publication surfaces
outputs.summary: Greenpill Paragraph, podcast notes, newsletter copy, and public Hub threads.

mandateParagraphs: The Writers Guild makes the network legible. It turns local experiments, chapter practice, books, podcasts, and builder work into public stories people can reuse.
The launch website should show stories as proof of activity and learning, not as a generic blog feed.
Draft newsletters and internal Drive notes should stay internal until a steward approves them for public publication.

cadence.summary: Public sources confirm Writers Guild activity and publication surfaces, but exact cadence needs steward confirmation before launch.

principles.title: Source-backed storytelling
principles.body: Every story should link to public proof or a steward-approved publication source.

principles.title: Field-first writing
principles.body: Lead with real chapter, guild, and program updates instead of abstract claims.

principles.title: No private drafts in public content
principles.body: Newsletter drafts, Drive docs, raw notes, and submission data stay out of public Keystatic until approved.

seo.title: Dev Guild · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Dev Guild builds open-source coordination tools for public goods, impact verification, and regenerative communities.

guilds.name: Dev Guild
guilds.summary: The engineering arm of Greenpill Network, building open-source coordination tools for chapters, funders, and regenerative communities.
guilds.description: The Dev Guild ships public tooling for impact measurement, capital allocation, community governance, reputation, and public network surfaces. Public sources connect the guild to Green Goods, GreenWill, Impact Reef, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Octant funding, Gitcoin rounds, and recurring builder programming.
guilds.oneliner: Building open-source coordination tools for the regenerative network.

outputs.label: Impact verification
outputs.summary: Green Goods and related tools help communities document and verify regenerative work.

outputs.label: Capital allocation
outputs.summary: Grant Ships, Cookie Jar, and related experiments explore public-goods funding mechanisms.

outputs.label: Reputation primitives
outputs.summary: GreenWill explores public-good reputation and pay-it-forward activity.

outputs.label: Public writing
outputs.summary: The Dev Guild Paragraph documents builder work and grant-facing updates.

mandateParagraphs: The Dev Guild is the engineering arm of Greenpill Network. It designs and ships open-source software that helps chapters and allied communities coordinate without relying on private intermediaries.
The strongest public proof points are Green Goods, GreenWill, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Impact Reef, public GitHub repositories, and the Octant / Gitcoin funding trail.

Where existing tools already do the job, the public direction should frame the guild as an integrator and steward of coordination infrastructure rather than a team rebuilding every primitive from scratch.

cadence.summary: Public sources confirm recurring builder programming, but exact call cadence should be steward-confirmed before launch copy promises a standing schedule.
cadence.format: Builder spaces and public async work

principles.title: Public by default
principles.body: Use public repositories, public docs, and public grant updates whenever they can carry the claim.

principles.title: Integrate before rebuilding
principles.body: Frame the guild around practical coordination infrastructure and integrations with tools such as EAS, Gitcoin, Allo, KarmaGAP, and Green Goods.

principles.title: Chapter-informed tooling
principles.body: Show how tools support local chapters and real regenerative work, not software for its own sake.

seo.title: Impact Reef · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Impact Reef is an experimental Greenpill Dev Guild project for qualitative public-good impact capture.

projects.name: Impact Reef
projects.summary: Qualitative public-good impact capture and evaluation tooling.
projects.description: Impact Reef is described in public Dev Guild project context as a tool for capturing qualitative data for public-good projects, backed by community endorsements and designed to help surface value to funders.

seo.description: The Greenpill Network V2 website rebuild grounds chapters, stories, library, guilds, Garden, map, and impact surfaces in real content.

projects.name: Grant Ships
projects.summary: Grant-allocation game and public-goods funding experiment.
projects.description: Grant Ships appears in the public Dev Guild funding/project inventory as an allocation game where funders compete to support projects and bring regenerative features into game ecosystems.

seo.title: Cookie Jar · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Cookie Jar is a Greenpill Dev Guild funding-pool project for DAOs and communities.

projects.name: Cookie Jar
projects.summary: Smart-contract-governed funding pools for DAOs and communities.
projects.description: Cookie Jar is listed in the Dev Guild public project surface as a funding primitive for communities: shared jars with allowlists, NFT gating, and configurable withdrawal rules.

seo.title: Regen Coordination Hub · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Regen Coordination Hub is Greenpill's public forum for chapter updates, proposals, and program coordination.

projects.name: Regen Coordination Hub
projects.summary: Public forum, chapter directory, and coordination surface for Greenpill and allied regen work.
projects.description: The Regen Coordination Hub is a public Discourse surface used for chapter updates, proposals, Greenpill Garden coordination, website revamp discussion, and network-level knowledge work. It appears at hub.regencoordination.xyz and hub.greenpill.network.

seo.title: Greenpill Impact Framework · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Impact Framework connects public impact verification, Green Goods, and regenerative capital coordination.

projects.name: Greenpill Impact Framework
projects.summary: Public impact verification and capital-formation framework connected to Green Goods.
projects.description: The public companion thread frames the Greenpill Impact Framework as a way to connect Green Goods v1, impact, strategy, measurement, coordination, and capital formation. Internal framework details shaped the research artifact, but launch copy should point to the public Hub thread unless stewards approve more.

media.imageAlt: Garden texture from the high-fidelity design package

seo.title: Greenpill Garden · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Greenpill Garden is a public onboarding and stewardship program connecting chapters, tools, and impact reporting.

projects.name: Greenpill Garden
projects.summary: Season-based public onboarding and stewardship program.
projects.description: Greenpill Garden Season One is a public program thread connecting stewardship, chapters, impact reporting, tools, and the website revamp. The public website should use it as the source-backed onboarding bridge while keeping private pledge and assessment state out of Keystatic.

seo.title: Greenpill Podcast · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Podcast is a long-running public media surface for regenerative crypto and public-goods coordination.

projects.name: Greenpill Podcast
projects.summary: Long-running public podcast about crypto-economic systems that create positive externalities.
projects.description: The Greenpill Podcast remains one of the network's strongest public media surfaces, with the research artifact citing 293 public episodes from 2022 through 2026 and sub-series including VDAO x Greenpill and Network Nations.

seo.title: GreenWill · Greenpill Network
seo.description: GreenWill is a Greenpill Dev Guild reputation and pay-it-forward tooling project.

projects.name: GreenWill
projects.summary: Public-good reputation and pay-it-forward activity tooling.
projects.description: GreenWill is described in the research source inventory as an open Farcaster mini-app for accumulating Greenpill and ReFi reputation from pay-it-forward activity such as Octant, Gitcoin, and sponsored rounds.

seo.title: Green Goods · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Green Goods is a Greenpill Dev Guild PWA for source-backed regenerative impact verification and public proof.

projects.name: Green Goods
projects.summary: Offline-first impact verification for regenerative work.
projects.description: Green Goods is a mobile-first PWA built by the Greenpill Dev Guild to document and verify ecological and social work. Public sources describe an offline-first flow where operators approve submissions and impact can be anchored with Ethereum Attestation Service on Arbitrum, with Green Goods positioned as core infrastructure for public proof.
